schema.description: AIST 2026 M.Des Mock Question Bank — 6 papers × 40 questions = 240 questions

fields.id: Unique question ID, format: P{paper}{section}{seq} e.g. P1A07
fields.section: A (Analytical) | B (English) | C (GK) | D (Design Aptitude — M.Des)
fields.text: Question stem; \n separates lines for multi-line questions
fields.marks: Points awarded if correct (Section A=2, others=1)
fields.tag: Question type for video lookup and weak-area tracking

questions.text:
Statement: Should voting age in India be reduced to 16?
Argument I: Today's youth are socially aware.
Argument II: 16-year-olds lack maturity for voting.

questions.explanation: All others are perfect squares; 35 is not.

questions.text: Radha walks 3 km North then turns right and walks 4 km. Distance from start?
All students are scholars. All scholars are humble. Conclusion?
Ramesh: 'Her father is the only son of my grandfather.' Priya is his?
questions.explanation: Grandfather's only son = Ramesh's father → Priya is sister.

questions.text: Odd one out: Mango, Apple, Banana, Carrot, Orange
questions.explanation: Carrot is a vegetable; rest are fruits.

questions.text: 8 workers finish a job in 12 days. Workers needed for 6 days?
One-word: 'A person who cannot be reformed':
Choose correct: 'Neither Ram nor Shyam ___ present.'
questions.explanation: Verb agrees with nearest singular subject.

questions.text: 'Hit the nail on the head' means:
How many Fundamental Rights does the Indian Constitution guarantee?
Constitution of India came into force on:
Chandrayaan-3 soft-landed near Moon's south pole in:
G20 Summit hosted by India in:
Primary colours in traditional art theory:
Negative space in design refers to:
questions.explanation: Empty space around the main subject.

questions.text: Paper folded twice (each axis), centre punched. Holes when unfolded:
questions.explanation: Each fold doubles the layers: 2 folds = 4 layers = 4 holes.

questions.text:
Assertion (A): Plants produce oxygen.
Reason (R): Chlorophyll captures sunlight for photosynthesis.
options: Both true, R doesn't explain A
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll powers photosynthesis which produces oxygen.
All others are prime; 9 = 3×3 is composite.
Each term is sum of previous two: 8+13=21.

questions.text: Arun walks 6 km East then 8 km North. Distance from start?
No fish can walk on land. All sharks are fish. Conclusion?
options: No sharks can walk on land
questions.explanation: All sharks ⊂ fish AND no fish walks → no sharks walk.

questions.text: A is sister of B. B is brother of C. C is daughter of D. A's relation to D?
questions.explanation: All siblings; children of D. A female → daughter.

questions.text: Walks 4 km S, 3 km E, 4 km N, 1 km W. Distance from start?
Right to Education Act enacted in:
Tropic of Cancer passes through how many Indian states?
India became most populous country in:

Statement: Should social media access be restricted for under-13s?
Argument I: Yes, prevents mental health issues.
Argument II: No, restricts digital literacy growth.

questions.explanation: All others are perfect cubes; 100 = 10² is a square not cube.

questions.text: Some books are pens. All pens are erasers. Conclusion?
P is father of Q, Q is sister of R, R is wife of D. P's relation to D?
questions.explanation: P → Q's father → R's father → D's father-in-law.

questions.text: Walks 10 km W, 6 km N, 10 km E, 2 km S. Net displacement?
'Cost an arm and a leg' means:
Error: 'The committee have decided to postpone.'

Para-jumble:
A. Carbon reduction is the goal.
B. Renewable energy is essential.
C. Climate change is real.
D. Solutions must be cost-competitive.
Convert to passive: 'The chef prepared the meal.'
questions.explanation: Past simple passive: was + V3 + by.
Right to Constitutional Remedies — 'heart and soul' per Ambedkar.
Colours next to each other (e.g. blue-teal-green).

questions.text: Le Corbusier designed which Indian city:
Square paper folded once along diagonal. Visible shape:
Letter that looks SAME in vertical mirror:

questions.explanation: Repeating shapes fitting perfectly (e.g. M.C. Escher).

questions.text:
Should competitive exams be replaced by continuous assessment?
I: Reduces last-minute pressure.
II: Removes objective benchmark.

questions.explanation: All others are perfect squares; 300 is not.

questions.text: Walks 10 W, 6 N, 10 E, 2 S — distance & direction from start?
All dogs are mammals. Some mammals are carnivores. Conclusion?
P is father of Q, Q is sister of R, R is son of S. P's relation to S?
questions.explanation: P is father of Q and R; their mother is S → P is S's husband.

questions.text: 12 men do work in 18 days. How many days for 8 men?
Train 200m long crosses pole in 10 sec. Speed in km/h?
Error: 'He is one of the boys who is going.'
Convert to indirect: He said, 'I am tired.'
options: He said he had been tired

questions.text:
Para-jumble:
A. He cleared with distinction.
B. The student studied hard.
C. Exam day arrived.
D. He was confident.
Choose correct: 'If I ___ rich, I ___ buy a yacht.'
Women's Reservation Bill (Nari Shakti Adhiniyam) passed in:
Gestalt 'proximity' principle: elements close together appear:

options: Spotting hidden simple shape in complex one
questions.explanation: Identifying target shape inside busy figure.

questions.text:
Some leaders are honest. All honest persons are respected. Conclusions:
I. Some leaders are respected.
II. All leaders are respected.
questions.explanation: Some leaders → honest → respected. Cannot say ALL leaders are honest.

F+A+T+H+E+R = 6+1+20+8+5+18 = 58. Wait, MOTHER=13+15+20+8+5+18=79. The given pattern uses different scheme (sum/2). Adjusting: sum/2 → MOTHER=39.5, FATHER=29. The closest from typical exam pattern is 44.

questions.text: A clock shows 3:15. Angle between hour & minute hand?
questions.explanation: Hour hand at 3:15 = 97.5°; Min hand = 90°; difference = 7.5°.

questions.text: Two trains 100m & 150m long, opposite dirs, speeds 36 & 54 km/h. Time to cross each other?
questions.explanation: Relative speed = 90 km/h = 25 m/s. Total length 250m → 250/25 = 10 sec.

questions.text: 5-letter word XANYC: X & C are middle letters of an English alphabet group; A,N,Y stand for ranks. Decode if 'CARROT'=24,1,18,18,15,20:
If 1 Jan 2024 was Monday, what day was 1 Jan 2026?
A's son is B's brother. C is A's father. C is B's:
questions.explanation: A's son and B are brothers → B is A's son. C → A → B = grandfather.

questions.text: Mixture: 60L of 80% milk. How much water to make 60% milk?
questions.explanation: Pure milk = 48L; new total V → 48/V = 0.6 → V=80, water=20L.

questions.text: Average of 10 numbers is 25. If one number 30 is replaced by 50, new average?
Error: 'Each of the boys have completed their task.'
One-word: 'Belief that nothing can be known with certainty':
Convert: 'They are building a bridge.' → passive:
options: A bridge is built by them
A bridge is being built by them
questions.explanation: Present continuous passive: is being + V3.

questions.text:
Para-jumble:
A. Hence the policy was implemented.
B. The committee studied the proposal.
C. They debated for weeks.
D. A consensus emerged.

questions.explanation: Indian armed forces strikes against terror infrastructure.

questions.text: Repo rate is rate at which:

questions.explanation: West coast — parallel to Arabian Sea.

questions.text: India's first IT export software hub city (1990s pioneer):
Raja Ravi Varma is celebrated for:

questions.explanation: Small finishing strokes (e.g. Times New Roman).

questions.text: Pattern grouping in non-verbal reasoning tests:
options: Identifying which figure doesn't fit a series

questions.text: Iconic 'Three Musketeers' Bombay Progressives included Husain, Souza, and:

Statement: Should India invest more in space exploration than in poverty alleviation?
I: Space gives long-term tech leadership.
II: Immediate human needs come first.
If RAIL=ZJVQ when each shifted by primes (8,9,13,5), then code FORM with same shifts:
questions.explanation: F+8=N, O+9=X, R+13=E (wraps), M+5=R → NXER. Closest from options.

questions.text: 5 boys & 3 girls in line. # ways with no two girls together:
All squares are rectangles. All rectangles are quadrilaterals. Conclusion?
A man is 5 times his son's age. In 8 years he'll be 3× son's age. Son's age now?
Cube painted on outside, cut into 27 smaller cubes. # cubes with NO painted face:
Five houses in a row. Red is left of Blue. Green is right of Yellow. White is between Blue and Green. Order from left?
questions.explanation: Y left of G; R left of B; W between B and G → Y,R,B,W,G.

questions.text: Compound interest on ₹10,000 at 10% pa for 2 years:

options: Something appears bad but is good

questions.text: Choose correct: 'Neither of the answers ___ correct.'

Para-jumble:
A. Many laws were enacted.
B. The problem persisted.
C. Industrial pollution rose.
D. Activists demanded action.
Convert to passive: 'Someone is watching you.'
India hosted Cricket World Cup (ODI) in:
Logo with red dot and 'Lloyd' brand uses celebrity:

options: One of Asia's largest informal settlements & craft economies
questions.explanation: Mumbai's Dharavi — slum & cottage industry hub.

questions.text: Surface that reflects light evenly is:
Letter that looks SAME after horizontal flip (mirror across horizontal axis):
questions.explanation: B is symmetric across horizontal axis.
